learning_outcomes.description: Outline the biology of plant-microbe & insect interactions
Outline the biology of plant interactions with their environment
Identify biotechnology applications of plant interactions
Critically evaluate literature in plant interactions
Demonstrate core practical skills in plant biology using laboratory and non-laboratory techniques to determine both qualitative and quantitative outcomes

assessments.task_description:
You will choose one topic to explore in depth from the list of supplied topics relating to plant, microbe, insect and environment interactions. Within this topic, you will then develop an annotated bibliography, with each one having a summary together with an evaluation of the academic metrics to attest to its relevance in the topic. Students must have a distinct topic from the list provided or identify their own for approval by the course coordinators. The topics must be chosen by the end of week 2 or a topic will be assigned.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.   

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

The use of AI or MT must be referenced appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Students may also refer to Library guides found under Additional assessment information > Acknowledging and referencing AI use.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You must submit your Assessment task, in Blackboard, via the Turnitin link by the submission deadline.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Prac 1- Quiz Week 3 - Week 3

Prac 2 - Quiz Week 5 - Week 5

Prac 3 - Quiz Week 7 - Week 7

Prac 4 - Skill Assessment Week 8 - Week 8

Prac 5 - Quiz Week 9 - Week 9

Prac 6 - Quiz and results submission Week 12 - Week 12
assessments.category: Notebook/ Logbook, Participation/ Student contribution, Practical/ Demonstration, Project, Quiz
assessments.task_description:
During the practical component of this course, you will isolate and identify a novel bacterium that can form a symbiosis with a legume using physiological, molecular, microbiological and bioinformatic approaches. In each class, you will complete an assessment that will be delivered by blackboard and/or demonstrate a laboratory technique by the end of each practical session. Blackboard assessment may include a mix of multiple-choice questions, short answer questions or uploading results from the practical session. Answers for questions may come from any of the practical classes, lectures, blackboard content or literature sources identified independently.

Attendance for the practical classes is mandatory to complete each sequential assessment. Due to the format of the assessment being completed during the practical session, where these activities are unable to be re-produced, extensions cannot be granted for Prac 1, 2, 3 and 5 assessment items.

For Prac 4 - Skill Assessment and Prac 6 - Quiz and results submission, extensions can be granted for approved students.

For Prac 4 and Prac 6 only, students who are unable to attend the practical for reasons beyond their control will need to complete the ‘Absence from Practical’ form (see below)

Students must be in attendance of at least 4 of the 6 practical classes to meet the Learning Objectives of this Assessment.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.   

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

The use of AI or MT must be referenced appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Students may also refer to Library guides found under Additional assessment information > Acknowledging and referencing AI use. 

Absence from a Practical Session

To obtain a mark for the practical classes students must participate and complete the associated exercises (submitting the practical report via Blackboard - Turnitin). If you do not participate in a practical class, or do not provide evidence of your attendance (i.e. signing attendance sheet), your report will not be marked/graded. Refer to late submission notes for more information. 

If you are unable to complete your timetabled practical/field trip session because of circumstances beyond your control (e.g., illness) you must submit a practical absence request immediately accompanied by acceptable supporting documentation.  

 A list of acceptable reasons and required supporting documentation is found at https://my.uq.edu.au/information-and-services/manage-my-program/exams-and-assessment/applying-assessment-extension?p=1#1  

Your request must be submitted no later than 24 hours following the missed practical/field trip. The submission link can be found in the Learning Resources (Blackboard Original) or Course Resources (Blackboard Ultra) folder in your course Blackboard site.  

Requests without the correct supporting documentation may not be approved. If the reason for your absence is not approved, you will be considered absent from that practical session.  

It is your responsibility to submit an application and provide correct documentation.  

  • Assessment extensions are only available for Pracs 4 and 6. In addition to the 'Absence from Practical' form, you must apply for an Extension of Assessment Due Date.
  • This course is unable to provide an alternative session to attend.

If you require further assistance, please contact the school - agriculture@enquire.uq.edu.au.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The online assessment is completed within Blackboard under the appropriate assessment title on your course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Throughout the course you will be presented with lectures on eight topics in Plant Interactions. While these topics may seem distinct, they are linked via various molecular, biochemical, and ecological, pathways as plants do not interact with single individual microorganisms, but rather a complex of biotic and abiotic factors at any given time. To help develop your conceptual understanding of these lecture topics, you will prepare a mind map that builds connections between the individual lectures.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.   

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.   

The use of AI or MT must be referenced appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Students may also refer to Library guides found under Additional assessment information > Acknowledging and referencing AI use.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You must submit your Assessment task, in Blackboard, via the Turnitin link by the submission deadline. You should also retain an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Weekly lectures

Lectures will be held at weekly intervals and will be delivered by UQ academics and guest lecturers linked to plant interactions with their environment. Students can discuss these topics with the speakers in order to explore the topic in depth and foster their knowledge and expertise across the subdisciplines. The content of these lectures will be assessable in Mind Map.

The content of these lectures will be assessable in Mind Map.

learning_activities.topic:
Practical classes

You will attend scheduled practical classes where you will isolate and identify a unique soil bacteria using microbiology, physiology, molecular biology and bioinformatic techniques. In each class, you will complete an assessment that will be delivered by blackboard and/or demonstrate a laboratory technique by the end of each practical session. Blackboard assessment may include a mix of multiple choice questions, short answer questions or uploading results from the practical session.

To obtain a mark for the practical classes students must participate and complete the associated exercises (submitting the practical report via Blackboard - Turnitin). If you do not participate in a practical class, or do not provide evidence of your attendance (i.e. signing attendance sheet), your report will not be marked/graded. Refer to late submission notes for more information. 

Absence from a Practical Session

If you are unable to complete your timetabled practical/field trip session because of circumstances beyond your control (e.g., illness) you must submit a practical absence request immediately accompanied by acceptable supporting documentation.  

 A list of acceptable reasons and required supporting documentation is found at https://my.uq.edu.au/information-and-services/manage-my-program/exams-and-assessment/applying-assessment-extension?p=1#1  

Your request must be submitted no later than 24 hours following the missed practical/field trip. The submission link can be found in the Learning Resources (Blackboard Original) or Course Resources (Blackboard Ultra) folder in your course Blackboard site.  

Requests without the correct supporting documentation may not be approved. If the reason for your absence is not approved, you will be considered as absent from that practical session.

It is your responsibility to submit an application and provide correct documentation.

This course is unable to provide an alternative session to attend.

All practical classes have an associated assessment item and you must apply for an extension of the assessment due date, noting that extensions are not available for assessments in Prac 1, 2, 3 and 5 .

If you require further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact the School - agriculture@enquire.uq.edu.au.
